metadata.title: Claude, Judged
metadata.color_semantics: claude: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream; code stays Onda dark glass
metadata.note: Audience-namespaced adaptation. Facts and visual argument preserved from the canonical reel. B08 states when to use and not use AI; B09 is the audience outro.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Start with the smallest gate: before spending on audio, the system checks one line, VERDICT: PASS. Determinable questions like schema validity and beat count become tests an agent answers reliably. Judgeable questions like whether the work is alive or fair remain a human responsibility. The agent inspects fields, durations, and QC frames, informing the final decision but never inheriting it.

artifactLines: Start with the smallest gate: before spending on audio, the system checks one line, VERDICT: PASS.
Determinable questions like schema validity and beat count become tests an agent answers reliably.
Judgeable questions like whether the work is alive or fair remain a human responsibility.
The agent inspects fields, durations, and QC frames, informing the final decision but never inheriting it.

beats.narration_text: I keep circling the split between determinable and judgeable questions, and I want to test where the line actually sits for my own pipeline. I'll list five checks my system runs before it spends money on audio. Can you help me (1) sort each check into determinable versus judgeable, (2) flag any I've secretly tried to automate that really need a human watching for boredom or unfairness, and (3) suggest one cheap evidence-leaving signal that could make a borderline judgeable check safer to gate on?

props.command: I keep circling the split between determinable and judgeable questions, and I want to test where the line actually sits for my own pipeline. I'll list five checks my system runs before it spends money on audio. Can you help me (1) sort each check into determinable versus judgeable, (2) flag any I've secretly tried to automate that really need a human watching for boredom or unfairness, and (3) suggest one cheap evidence-leaving signal that could make a borderline judgeable check safer to gate on?

beats.narration_text: Start with the smallest possible gate. Before generating audio, the system looks for one line: VERDICT: PASS. It does not understand the whole film. It answers one narrow question with complete reliability. There is something beautiful in giving a test no more authority than it has earned.

props.title: GATE P — generate_audio.py
props.code:
ped = Path(slug_dir) / 'PEDAGOGY.md'
if not re.search(
    r'^\s*VERDICT:\s*PASS\b', ped.read_text()
):
    print('[err] GATE P: no VERDICT: PASS')
    sys.exit(3)
# cheap. deterministic. correct.

beats.narration_text: Now two kinds of questions appear. Some are determinable: does the schema validate, do nine beats exist, is the file where it should be? Others are judgeable: is the idea alive, is the pacing humane, should this be published? The first kind becomes a test. The second remains a responsibility.

props.heading: Determinable vs Judgeable
props.body: Some questions can be tested: schema, beat count, file paths. Others must be decided: is the idea alive, is the pacing humane, should this be published?

beats.narration_text: A cheap gate protects the expensive act of judgment. It can stop a bad plan before voices are generated and hours are spent. But it cannot watch on our behalf. The final cut still has to meet a person who can notice boredom, confusion, delight, and consequence.

props.heading: Cheap gates, expensive judgment
props.body: A gate stops the audio spend before voices are generated. But it cannot watch on our behalf. Judgment still needs a person.

beats.narration_text: An agent can inspect the things that leave evidence: fields, durations, paths, resolution, and QC frames. These are not lesser tasks. They are the structure that lets attention move toward the harder question instead of being wasted on preventable mistakes.

props.heading: What agents can inspect
props.body: Fields, durations, paths, resolution, QC frames — things that leave evidence. These tasks free attention for the harder question.

beats.narration_text: What should never disappear into automation is the decision that carries meaning: whether the work is good, whether it earns the viewer's time, whether its simplifications are fair, whether it should leave the workshop. The agent can inform that decision. It cannot inherit it.

props.heading: What must not be automated
props.body: Whether the work is good, whether it earns the viewer's time, whether its simplifications are fair. The agent informs. It cannot inherit the decision.

beats.narration_text: The series keeps returning to the same architecture. Route models by cost. Expand trust only after evidence. Freeze hard-won judgment into skills. Pin exact instructions when variation is dangerous. Treat outputs as distributions. Condition them deliberately. Then ask who signs. Each move places uncertainty where it can be seen.

props.heading: The architecture of the series
props.body: Route by cost. Expand trust after evidence. Freeze judgment into skills. Pin exact instructions. Treat outputs as distributions. Condition deliberately. Then ask who signs.

beats.narration_text: So the boundary is not human versus machine. It is evidence versus judgment. Let machines check what can be demonstrated: schema, timing, paths, resolution. Let humans decide what must be answered for: interest, care, and publication. The most capable system may be the one that knows where its capability ends.

artifactLines: Agents check the determinable: schema, timing, paths, resolution.
Humans check the judgeable: interesting, worth watching, publish.
Gates are cheap taste-checks before expensive ones.
The determinism boundary is the human-judgment boundary.

beats.narration_text: When should we use AI here? Use it where a mistake can be named, tested, and corrected: missing files, broken timing, inconsistent structure. Do not use it to surrender the question of meaning: whether the work is honest, worthwhile, or ready to meet another person. The boundary is not a failure of intelligence. It is where responsibility begins.

props.artifactTitle: When to use AI — and when not to
artifactLines: USE AI — explicit evidence · reversible checks · repeatable rules
DO NOT DELEGATE — meaning · fairness · worth · publication
The machine reports. A person remains answerable.

beats.narration_text: Follow the question further at Medhavy.com.
